title: The Last Tram
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A tram driver on Lisbon's oldest line discovers that his midnight route passes through a version of the city that ceased to exist in 1955.
long_description:
Jorge Mendes has driven Lisbon's oldest tram line for eighteen years. At midnight on the last Sunday of every month, his route passes through a shadow version of the city -- Lisbon as it existed in 1955, complete with cobblestone streets, black-market cafes, and the electric hum of a world that history forgot.

When Jorge falls for Maria, a woman from the lost city, he discovers that she is bound to die in 1957 -- a fact he cannot change, only witness. The Night Conductor, the enigmatic gatekeeper of the time-slip, warns him that every crossing has a cost. But Jorge cannot stay away.

The Last Tram is a slipstream novel about love across time, the weight of memory, and the streets of a Lisbon that exists only in the space between midnight and dawn. It asks whether love can survive the end of the world -- even when that world has already ended.
